Combined Sewer Overflows - Part 2

Monitoring Strategies for Prevention

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

Preventing Overflow Events

The key to preventing combined sewer overflows is ensuring critical pumping equipment operates reliably. Condition monitoring allows utilities to identify and address problems before they cause failures.

Monitoring Approach

Samotics monitors the electrical signatures of pump motors in combined sewer systems. By detecting early signs of motor degradation, bearing wear, and seal leakage, utilities can schedule maintenance proactively.

Real Results

Water utilities using predictive maintenance have achieved:

  • 50%+ reduction in unplanned downtime
  • Extended equipment lifespan
  • Reduced maintenance costs
  • Better environmental compliance

Conclusion

By implementing condition monitoring for critical sewer infrastructure, utilities protect both the environment and their operational budgets. Predictive maintenance transforms a reactive cost center into a proactive risk mitigation strategy.
